name: Sendero del Cura Merino (PRC-BU-124) — Fontioso circular desde Villoviado
region_intro: Hiking around Lerma offers trails through the Arlanza river valley and surrounding countryside. The landscape features holm oak forests, agricultural fields, and gentle hills, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. The region's trails often follow riverbanks or wind through rural areas, connecting small villages. Best hiking trails around Lerma The most popular hiking route is Río Arlanza Riverside Trail, an easy 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route follows the Arlanza river, offering level paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero de las Grandes Encinas (PRC-BU 121) — circular desde Solarana, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This loop hike around Lerma leads through significant holm oak woodlands. Local hikers also love the El Montecillo Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through rolling hills and rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Lerma is defined by river valleys, holm oak forests, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, including family friendly hikes Lerma. The routes in Lerma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 16 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Lerma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lerma?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Lerma, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Lerma?
A: Yes, Lerma offers several easy trails perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040977277/rio-arlanza-riverside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.3 km) long and follows the Arlanza river on level paths.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes near Lerma?
A: Yes, Lerma has several excellent circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281903963/sendero-de-las-grandes-encinas-prc-bu-121-circular-desde-solarana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Grandes Encinas (PRC-BU 121) — circular desde Solarana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.9-mile (9.4 km) loop through holm oak woodlands. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292353728/sendero-del-cura-merino-prc-bu-124-fontioso-circular-desde-villoviado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Cura Merino (PRC-BU-124) — Fontioso circular desde Villoviado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in the Lerma area?
A: Many of the trails around Lerma are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040977277/rio-arlanza-riverside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice, offering gentle terrain along the river that is enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Lerma?
A: Hiking around Lerma offers diverse landscapes, including the Arlanza river valley, extensive holm oak forests, and agricultural plains. You'll encounter gentle hills and rural areas, often connecting small villages.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks to see near the hiking trails?
A: Along and near the hiking trails, you can explore several interesting landmarks. Don't miss the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Ducal Palace of Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;. Nature lovers might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lerma?
A: The hiking experience in Lerma is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Hikers often praise the region's varied terrain, from riverside paths to holm oak woodlands, and the opportunity to explore rural landscapes and charming villages.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views or scenic overlooks?
A: Yes, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292732959/el-montecillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Montecillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; lead through rolling hills, providing scenic views of the surrounding rural landscapes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated viewpoint offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options around Lerma?
A: While many routes are day hikes, some trails offer longer distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040988412/rio-arlanza-y-villahoz-circular-desde-tordomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza y Villahoz — circular desde Tordómar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.8-mile (17.4 km) circular route that provides a more extended hiking experience along the river and through villages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lerma?
A: The best time for hiking in Lerma is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be warm, especially in the open plains, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Arlanza river?
A: Yes, the Arlanza river is a central feature of the region's hiking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040977277/rio-arlanza-riverside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering an easy walk directly along its banks. Other routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040988412/rio-arlanza-y-villahoz-circular-desde-tordomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza y Villahoz — circular desde Tordómar&lt;/a&gt;, also incorporate sections along the river.
tips: The town of the famous Cura Merino, guerrilla during the War of Independence. In the town we can see his beautiful house and the parish church of San Vítores. The door that enters the church is decorated with a cheer in memory of the exploits of Cura Merino. The church is dedicated to San Vítores, a saint from Burgos who died beheaded. Next to the church is the Moral de Villoviado, a unique tree in the province of Burgos.
The town's name owes its origin to the fact that its lands were home to "fountains and bears." It has an 18th century church with the statue of Saint Columba, patron saint of the town.
